item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, the tone on this one just hurts. Has not aged well. The generally uninteresting effect doesn’t help much, either. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see how this could be Euclid to some people, but is it really so bad as to rarely not be followed by paranoia? Of course, maybe I've just got a high tolerance to being creeped by things that won't intentionally harm me.
Also, would it be impossible to get a test on the long-term affects of an imprinting? Say, getting it bound to a Class D for the majority or entirety of their time at the Foundation? Because they've clearly conducted experiments, but by the notes, don't really go beyond the course of a couple days, a week at most…
Don't get me wrong, I love this, just a couple ideas I had.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Edits made. Timer removed. See below for more details.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so here we have a little ball that imprints on people and follows them around. I don't know about you, but if I was a researcher for the Foundation I'd want to know if such imprinting had a genetic basis. A way to do that would be to have the object imprint on a subject, then present the sphere with a genetically identical subject (aka clone) to see if there are any changes in its behavior. Good thing we have a basically unlimited supply of clones in the instances of SCP-1680. Recommending the following edits:

Editing the following:

Whenever a human touches SCP-104 in its “Unimprinted” state, it will “Imprint” on the subject. If the imprinted subject moves more than 9 m (30 ft) from SCP-104 for more than five minutes, SCP-104 will appear within 2 m (7 ft) of the imprinted subject. This shift appears instantaneous, disappearing and reappearing in less than a nanosecond, regardless of distance or intervening objects. Every hour that the subject remains within 9 m (30 ft) of SCP-104, it will move one foot closer to the subject until it is touching the subject. When SCP-104 is touching its imprinted subject, it will not attempt to move closer. SCP-104 will not exhibit this behavior to non-imprinted subjects and will only imprint on one subject at a time. Thick cloth or metal appears to block the imprinting in most cases.

To this:

Whenever a human touches SCP-104 in its “Unimprinted” state, it will “Imprint” on the subject. If the imprinted subject moves more than 9 m (30 ft) from SCP-104 for more than five minutes, SCP-104 will appear within 2 m (7 ft) of the imprinted subject. This shift appears instantaneous, disappearing and reappearing in less than a nanosecond, regardless of distance or intervening objects. Every hour that the subject remains within 9 m (30 ft) of SCP-104, it will move one foot closer to the subject until it is touching the subject. When SCP-104 is touching its imprinted subject, it will not attempt to move closer. SCP-104 will not exhibit this behavior to non-imprinted subjects and will only imprint on one subject at a time. Thick cloth or metal appears to block the imprinting in most cases. Testing of SCP-104 with multiple instances of SCP-1680 suggests this imprinting is not genetically based, as SCP-104 does not change behaviors when faced with multiple genetically identical subjects.

I don't want to speak for Gears as to how the object actually functions, so having there be no change in behavior when presented with the 1680 instances leaves a lot of mystery behind.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: I think it's cute.
item.content:encoded:
It just wants a friend! I'd keep it in my pocket and call it my lucky charm.

I'd upvote, but I think the containment procedures are way over the top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one quite a lot. Though I think the containment procedures are too complex. They need not be more complex than 050's. Imprint it on someone and just leave it there. Ask for volunteers from D Class and offer exemption from monthly mind wipes/ overtly hazardous duties. At that point it would be "safe", being that it couldn't be re-imprinted and you could always lock the guy literally in a box.

+1. Would +more if I was able.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to have to agree that this thing seems like a Safe to me. The SCP doesn't understand the exact workings of most SCPs. I'm a fan of the locked box test, and when unimprinted this can literally just be left in a box and ignored, plus when imprinted it had very predictable non-threatening (if annoying) behavior. It's otherwise a cute enough concept - I really like seeing SCPs that aren't apocalypses in the making.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"And the cat ball came back, the very next day.
Yes, the cat ball came back. They thought it was a goner,
but the cat ball came back, it just wouldn't stay away!"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i feex

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Object class is generally based on both factors, danger and containment difficulty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Object class isn't primarily based on danger, but how difficult it is to contain. Since the thing is autonomous, it makes sense that it's Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me smile. But shouldn’t it be Safe? I mean, it’s not life-threatening or anything…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
unimaginably complex

…really?

Not enough to this, either. Could be a lot more important, as the idea has some interesting potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D'aww, the ball wants a friend :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not just keep the imprinted person around as SCP-104-1 ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I said nothing about personnel staying in the coma. It just seems to me that, unless said personnel is of value to the Foundation, terminating them would be the easiest way. Obviously, anyone of use will probably be put in a coma-like state and revived after SCP-104 has unimprinted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It states that you need to *enter* a near-death state, not that you have to STAY in one. You could probably get put into a medically induced coma for a few days, your vitals next to nothing, brought back up (hopefully) and eventually recover. At least from the exposure to 104 that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if unlikely or…

Anyone found to be “imprinted” by SCP-104 must submit for “unimprinting”. Failure to do so within 24 hours of exposure will result in termination.

SCP-104 can only be “unimprinted” if the subject is killed or enters a near-death like state.

So either personnel accidentally having been imprinted on are put in what amounts to a coma or they're killed. With my delicate sensibilities, I'm choosing to believe the latter. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unlikely. "Report for termination or you will be terminated". Hm. Decisions, decision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As its workings or effects aren't understood yet, I think Euclid is the best choice for class. And as the imprinted SCP-104 follows someone around indefinitely, unimprinting is essential to keeping it contained. Unless you imprint it on someone and stick him/her in the cell with it.

Actually, I love how you're probably shot on sight if you've imprinted, under the guise of 'unimprinting'. Euphemism of the century.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't this actually safe?

Also, considering it isn't harmful you really don't need to be killed/near-killed to release it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It appears to be the physical body. Not a easy task, but if you were able to ditch your physical body, it would stay bonded to it while you went on to…whatever. A extreme example, but possible.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
Does it imprint on the body or the spirit/soul?

I wonder what would happen if somebody tried to imprint it on Dr. Bright or Kain Pathos Crow…Or perhaps one of the human-looking-but-not SCPs.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
1: If you destroy it while unimprinted, it would be more or less disabled. However, if you were to touch any of the shards, it would imprint on you. Then, if you moved too far away, it would blip back to you, whole and undamaged.

2: So far, it appears to only imprint on humans. No other mammals, not even higher apes, appear to trigger it, but testing is ongoing. If someone is imprinted, then infected with SCP-217, SCP-104 will continue to follow them until they are approximately 75% "converted" by SCP-217, at which point it will unimprint.

3: It still follows them, no matter what. they could be in a situation where SCP-104 is immediately destroyed the instant it appears, and it will just keep appearing, over and over again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens to 104 if you've smashed it, blasted it, whatever; while it's unimprinted? Would this effectively neutralize it?
If not, when imprinted, will it follow whole or in pieces?

What criteria must something meet in order to be imprinted? Could a flea be imprinted? What happens if a subject is imprinted, and then infected with SCP-217?

How does 104 react to imprinted subjects skydiving? Or in the vaccum of space?

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Eventually it will pass through your system, and unless you keep the poo with SCP-104 in it around you at all times, it will blip back whole and undamaged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you smash it, grind it up, and eat it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like people who ask the hard questions…

It does nothing. It just touches you, and stays there until you move, in which case it starts following you. I need to do an edit as well, I think, as it appears I was unclear about the distances. If you move more than ten yards away, it will blip to within seven feet of you. If you are inside that ten yard radius, it will keep blipping one foot towards you every hour.

I guess the thing I was shooting for with this was paranoia. This thing will be with you forever, until you die or come damn close. Work, school, dates, anywhere, this thing will be with you. How do you explain that? It could start to work your nerves after a while.

This is one I tried hard on, but I feel I missed the mark. I wanted to make a Safe item that was still creepy and unsettling. If people think it’s a good idea, I’ll run an edit on it and see if I can’t make it a little more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what does SCP 104 DO after it touches a person? Just sit there? And can't it easily be defeated by moving every seven hours? It comes 1 foot closer every hour, so as long as you're not a heavy sleeper you can toss it/kick it/walk away and it'll teleport to the 7 foot distance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it will just disappear and reappear, and not take the item with it. it doesn't really "move", just sort of "blips" from place to place, so i don't see it taking the wall or anything with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a couple questions, if anyone can answer them:

If you affix an object to 104, and wait for 104 to move, will 104 have the object on it when it reappears?
If you affix 104 to a wall, will it move? If so, will it bring any of the wall with it?
